ground_truth.description: Train street in Hanoi. Train tracks through street instead of cars, Vietnamese flag and typical Asian street should give it away.
ground_truth.photographer_description: Train Street in Hanoi. The train tracks going through the street instead of cars, along with a Vietnamese flag and typical Asian street should give it away. You can see cafe chairs along the tracks. Nighttime lit streets, clear view.

details.description: Nighttime scene of Hanoi’s famous Train Street: a single railway track runs down the center of a very narrow lane lined with two- to three-story shophouses converted into cafes and bars. Warm yellow facades, balconies, and open-front eateries sit only a few feet from the rails. The street is festooned with strings of colorful paper lanterns, neon signs, and fairy lights. Vietnamese national flags hang overhead. People are walking, sitting on stools, and taking photos along the ballast beside the track. Signage in Vietnamese and English advertises coffee, cocktails, and Vietnamese/vegetarian food. A bicycle, potted plants, and small tables line the right side. The track curves gently away into the distance under dark night skies, illuminated by the ambient lights of the cafes.

details.description: Night scene of a narrow street in Hanoi with a railway track running down the middle. On both sides, low-rise shophouses host cafés and eateries, their façades lit by warm yellow lights. Colorful lanterns and string lights hang overhead, adding a festive ambiance. People stroll along the sides and some sit at outdoor tables. The gravel ballast and rails are clearly visible, giving the impression of a working railway embedded in an urban street. The scene resembles Hanoi's famous Train Street in the Old Quarter, with the track curving away into the distance between tightly packed buildings.

details.description: Nighttime street scene in Hanoi where an active railway track curves tightly through a narrow alley lined with brightly lit cafes and bars. Colorful lanterns, neon signs, and Vietnamese flags hang above the track, while pedestrians and patrons stand close to the rails, taking photos and socializing. The setting is the famous Hanoi Train Street, where trains pass within meters of buildings in the Old Quarter.

details.description: Night scene on a narrow Hanoi street where a railway track runs down the middle between closely packed storefronts and cafés. Warm lights and colorful lanterns hang overhead, with bright signage in Vietnamese on both sides. Pedestrians stand or sit along the edges of the track, and a few bicycles are visible. The buildings are low-rise and the street is lined with outdoor seating, creating a lively, tourist-friendly atmosphere typical of Hanoi’s old quarter. The setting strongly resembles the famous Train Street near Ly Nam De street in Hoan Kiem district, Hanoi, Vietnam.

details.description: A vibrant night scene at the famous Hanoi Train Street, where a single railway track runs through a narrow residential and commercial corridor. The scene is illuminated by warm artificial lighting, colorful string lights, and numerous traditional lanterns. On the left, a prominent two-story yellow building houses the 'Ga Dong Duong' cafe (Indochina Station), featuring balconies with patrons, red lanterns, and signage advertising Vietnamese food and coffee. The right side of the street is equally lively, with various shops and cafes decorated with bright lights and a Vietnamese flag hanging overhead. The ground is covered in railway ballast (gravel), and tourists can be seen walking along the tracks or sitting at low tables placed dangerously close to the rails. A dental clinic sign is visible on the far right.

details.description: A vibrant night scene of the famous Hanoi Train Street, where a functional railway track runs through a narrow residential and commercial corridor. The buildings on either side are packed closely to the tracks, many converted into cafes and restaurants like 'Ga Đông Dương' (Indochina Station), which is prominently featured with its yellow facade and red lanterns. The street is illuminated by numerous colorful traditional Vietnamese lanterns, fairy lights, and neon signs. People are seen walking along the tracks and sitting at outdoor cafe tables. A Vietnamese flag hangs from one of the buildings. The ground is covered in grey railway ballast surrounding the steel rails and concrete sleepers.

details.description: Nighttime view of Hanoi’s famous ‘Train Street’: a narrow lane with an active single railway track running down the center, flanked tightly by colorful cafés and bars in tall, narrow shophouses. Warm yellow façades, balconies, and open seating line both sides. Numerous hanging lanterns, string lights, neon signs (in Vietnamese and English), and multiple Vietnamese national flags decorate the street. The rail bed is exposed ballast with steel rails curving slightly through the scene. Crowds of visitors stand and sit along the edges of the track, typical of this tourist spot in Hanoi’s Old Quarter.

details.description: Night street scene in Hanoi with a single railway track running down the middle of a pedestrian-friendly alley. On both sides are storefronts and cafes with outdoor seating, illuminated by warm yellow lights and a profusion of colorful decorative lanterns and strings of lights overhead. Signs in Vietnamese line the buildings, and a distant sign appears to read Hanoi. Bicycles lean against walls and people stroll or sit at the cafes, creating a lively nightlife atmosphere. The track bed is laid with gravel and concrete sleepers, curving slightly as it recedes into the distance, giving the impression of a famous 'Train Street' setting.

details.description: Nighttime street scene with a single railroad track running down the center of a very narrow urban alley lined with brightly lit cafés and shops. Strings of paper lanterns, neon signs, and festive lights hang over the track. People sit and stand on the edges of the ballast and platforms beside the rails, while a red Vietnamese flag is visible above. The track curves gently away into the distance; the buildings are close enough that passing trains would brush by within inches. The atmosphere suggests a popular tourist spot where an active rail line passes through a residential/commercial lane.

details.description: Night street scene in Vietnam featuring a narrow pedestrian street bisected by a railway track. On both sides are lit storefronts, cafes, and restaurants with outdoor seating. The street is festooned with colorful lanterns and string lights overhead, creating a vibrant, festive atmosphere. People walk along the sidewalks and a bicycle is visible on the right; the rails lead the eye toward a distant vanishing point where the street narrows between buildings.

details.description: A vibrant nighttime view looking down the tracks of the famous Hanoi Train Street in Vietnam. A single railway track runs centrally through a narrow urban canyon lined with multi-story buildings converted into cafes, restaurants, and shops. The scene is festively illuminated with strings of warm lights, colorful paper lanterns (red, yellow, green), and neon signs. On the left, a bright yellow two-story building features a balcony with patrons and signage for 'Vietnamese Foods' and 'Coffee - Cocktail'. A prominent sign reading 'GA ĐÔNG DƯƠNG' with a train logo hangs above the street. Further down, a red neon 'HANOI' sign is visible. On the right, a 'BEST DENTAL' sign and a Vietnamese flag (red with yellow star) are displayed. People are walking directly on the railway sleepers and ballast, while others sit at low tables placed along the edges of the tracks.

entities.name: Hanoi Train Street
entities.notes: Famous tourist attraction where trains pass through a narrow residential street

details.description: A nighttime view of the famous Hanoi Train Street in Vietnam. A single set of railway tracks runs through a very narrow residential and commercial alleyway, laid on a bed of grey gravel. On both sides, narrow multi-story buildings house cafes, bars, and restaurants. The scene is brightly lit with numerous red lanterns, strings of fairy lights, and neon signs. People are seen sitting at small tables on the narrow sidewalks or standing near the tracks. A prominent yellow building on the left features a sign for 'Ga Đông Dương' restaurant. A Vietnamese flag hangs from a building on the right. The atmosphere is lively and crowded, typical of this popular tourist destination.
